methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RIOS Intelligent Machines is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Bernard Casse that has raised $46M+ in total capital. The valuation landscape for frontier robotics remains fragmented: the broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes 1 public comparable (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median approach cannot be meaningfully applied here. While 11 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy narrow stage-matching or valuation-tier filters simultaneously—the minimum threshold of three datapoints was not met. Restricting analysis to same-stage or adjacent-stage robotics companies of comparable size yielded zero qualified matches, rendering sector-wide median calculations unreliable without risking material distortion.

Given the absence of sufficient tight comparables and the inapplicability of standard triangulation methods, any valuation estimate would rest on incomplete public market reference data and qualitative positioning relative to the fragmented private peer set. The company's $46M+ raised provides a floor estimate but does not establish enterprise value. Key uncertainties include the trajectory of robotics commercialization, margin profiles of actual peer cohorts, and whether the public comp (277810.KQ) represents a relevant benchmark for this stage and technology focus. A credible range would require either deeper disclosure of peer-stage metrics or transaction-based reference points not present in the available data.
